events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need return exactly one valid JSON object matching requested schema. User asks compliance check. Need decide pass/fail. Scenario: commercial construction, Panelboard installation & makeup, rough-complete, observed field condition: double-tapped breakers (non-listed). Work roughed in. Reference NEC Art. 408, 110.26, 200.4. Double-tapped breakers (non-listed) means two conductors under a single breaker terminal not listed/approved. NEC violation: Art. 110.3(B) installation per listing/instructions; 408.36 maybe overcurrent protection. Also 200.4? That's multiwire branch circuits maybe neutral termination. But double tapped breakers non-listed fail. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk high due fire/hazard. Percent complete maybe 75. value irrelevant 0. Sound etc default. workable empty. findings: double-tapped breakers without listed connectors. actions: remove/replace breakers with listed dual-rated breakers or add pigtails/terminal connectors. rationale. references: NEC 110.3(B), 408.36, maybe 110.26 working clearance if relevant but double tapping doesn't directly. Use Art. 408 (panelboard requirements), 110.26. Need output raw JSON no prose. Ensure fields present.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Circuit breakers have two conductors terminated under a single terminal (double-tapped) when the breaker is not listed for multiple conductors.",
    "Double-tapped terminations can cause loose connections, overheating, arcing, and fire risk."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the double-tapped conductors and reinstall using breakers listed for two conductors, add listed splices/pigtails to a single conductor per terminal, or replace with factory-approved terminal accessories per manufacturer instructions.",
    "Verify all terminations are torqued to manufacturer specifications and re-inspect before energizing."
  ],
  "rationale": "Non-listed double-tapped breaker terminations violate the NEC requirement that equipment be installed and used in accordance with its listing and the manufacturer's instructions. This creates a defect and fire hazard, so the panelboard makeup fails compliance at rough-complete.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "NEC Art. 408",
    "NEC 110.26"
  ]
}
